business.headline: SpaceX opens at $150 in strong Nasdaq debut, up 11% from IPO priceSpaceX surged more than 20 per cent in its Nasdaq debut, lifting its valuation above USD 2 trillion as investors piled into the world's biggest IPO. The stock opened at USD 150, signalling strong demand and easing concerns over its market debut.

Groceries, fuel, school fees: Is your monthly budget about to get tighter?School fees, petrol, vegetables, utility bills—the list of monthly expenses never seems to end. As inflation rises, many families are asking the same question: is their budget about to get even tighter?

Inflation rises to 3.93% for May as West Asia conflict pushes up fuel and food pricesFood inflation continued to move higher in May. It stood at 4.78%, up from 4.20% in April, showing that prices of food items are rising faster than they were a month earlier.

Markets rally on easing West Asia tensions, oil falling below $88The S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Salary mismatch to missed interest: 7 ITR mistakes that can trigger tax noticesFiling your ITR may seem straightforward, but even a minor reporting error can attract the attention of the tax department. Here are seven mistakes you should watch out for.

The world's biggest IPO starts trading today. Is Elon Musk's SpaceX overvalued?SpaceX begins trading on Nasdaq after a record $75 billion IPO and a $1.75 trillion valuation. The debut has sharpened questions over valuation, Musk's control, xAI losses and broader market liquidity.

FD rates up to 8.10%: Check these small finance banks offering the highest returnsIn a world of rising uncertainty, guaranteed returns are becoming more valuable. Here's a look at the small finance banks offering FD rates as high as 8.10% this month.

Govt's new petrol, diesel order: Who is affected, and why was it introduced?The Centre has restricted industrial and commercial bulk fuel purchases from retail pumps. The move aims to protect pump supplies for ordinary consumers amid a wide retail-bulk price gap.

Earning Rs 20 lakh? Claim these tax benefits under the new tax regimeThink the new tax regime leaves no room for tax savings? If you earn Rs 20 lakh a year, you may be surprised to learn that several tax-friendly benefits are still available.
Gold demand picks up despite PM's appeal to avoid buyingAt the time of writing at 11.45 am, MCX gold was trading at Rs 1,49,942, up Rs 1,021, while silver stood at Rs 2,42,232, higher by Rs 2,579.

India may let fiscal deficit widen as US-Iran war raises oil costsIndia may allow its fiscal deficit to widen to 4.8% of GDP as the Iran war raises oil-linked costs. The move reflects pressure from higher subsidies, costlier imports and possible spending cuts.

Inflation fears return: Will Indians have to spend more on everyday items?Rising global oil prices and stronger US inflation are reviving concerns over India's household costs. Economists and the RBI say risks are building even without an immediate inflation shock.

Sensex jumped 1,700 points: 3 reasons why stock market rose todayThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 1,695.40 points, or 2.30%, to close at 75,527.95, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 461.30 points, or 1.99%, to settle at 23,622.90.

Markets surge as crude oil falls below $90 amid hopes of US-Iran breakthroughThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 873.88 points, or 1.18%, to 74,706.43 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 climbed 236.40 points, or 1.02%, to 23,398.00.

World's first trillionaire Elon Musk is now richer than Taiwan after SpaceX IPOElon Musk became the world's first trillionaire after SpaceX's record IPO lifted his holdings. The milestone pushed his fortune past Taiwan's economy and widened the gap with every other billionaire.

Centre restricts bulk industrial fuel purchases at petrol pumpsThe government has barred industrial, commercial and institutional users from buying petrol and diesel at retail pumps. The move seeks to prevent diversion, curb shortages and protect supplies for ordinary vehicle owners.

US drug agency puts Dabur products on import alert after inspection flags lapsesThe US FDA has placed drugs manufactured at Dabur India's Dadra and Nagar Haveli factory under an import alert after an inspection found data integrity and maintenance lapses.

Steady adjustment of rupee is okay, a free fall is not: Raghuram RajanIn an exclusive interview with India Today TV, former Reserve Bank of India Governor Raghuram Rajan shares his thoughts on how to deal with the ongoing conflict in West Asia's impact on the global and Indian economies and more.

Something is off: Raghuram Rajan questions India's growth narrative"Something is off" with India's economy, former RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan said, questioning whether official GDP figures showing growth of more than 7 per cent accurately reflect conditions on the ground.

India to stay world's fastest-growing major economy, growth at 6.6%: World BankIndia will remain the world's fastest-growing major economy, with growth expected to slow from 7.7% in FY2025-26 to 6.6% in FY2026-27 before recovering to 7.2% the following year.

entertainment.headline: Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata box office Day 1: Kangana Ranaut film opens on slow note Actor Kangana Ranaut's Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata opened with modest collections on a crowded Friday at the box office. Its weekend run will now hinge on word-of-mouth in a nine-film clash.

Haunted 3D review: Familiar ghost story that works better than expectedRelease Date:Sun Jul 12, 2026 Vikram Bhatt's Haunted 3D: Echoes of the Past follows a betrayed director into a cursed palace and a ghostly romance. The film's mystery holds attention, but weak VFX and minimal scares limit its impact.

Aamir Khan's Lagaan team reunites ahead of film's 25th anniversary Actor Aamir Khan and filmmaker Ashutosh Gowariker reunited with several members of the Lagaan cast ahead of the film's 25th anniversary. The gathering celebrated the legacy of the 2001 classic.

Ahaan Panday, Aneet Padda's matching star tattoos spark fan frenzy Fresh pictures of Ahaan Panday and Aneet Padda have sparked talk of matching neck tattoos. The viral sighting has intensified fan frenzy around the Saiyaara pair and their next film together.

India Ke Top 1% promo out: Anil Kapoor hunts for bright minds Star Plus has released the first promo of India Ke Top 1%, hosted by Anil Kapoor. The teaser presents the show as a logic-led contest that rewards common sense and calm under pressure.

Welcome to the Jungle will hopefully be another Khakee for me: Tusshar Kapoor Actor Tusshar Kapoor expressed hope that Welcome to the Jungle could be another Khakee moment in his career. He said director Ahmed Khan's handling of the giant cast gives every character room to stand out.

Dhamaal 4 trailer: Ajay Devgn rides flying dolphins, iconic cast teases comic chaos The Dhamaal 4 trailer introduces Ajay Devgn and the ensemble cast in a chaotic treasure hunt centred on a mountain of gold. It signals a bigger-scale return for the franchise as it begins its run-up to the film release.

Taylor Swift inducted into Songwriters Hall of Fame, thanks family in tearful speech Taylor Swift thanked her family and Steven Spielberg as she accepted her Songwriters Hall of Fame honour in New York. The speech framed her record-setting induction at 36 as a reflection on sacrifice, storytelling and endurance.

Box office: Governor vs Main Vaapas vs Bharat Bhhagya and why Haunted 3D is surprise The industry witnessed an unusually crowded box-office clash on Friday as nine films hit theatres simultaneously. With Governor, Main Vaapas Aaunga and Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata leading the race, trade experts believe the weekend winner will ultimately be decided by word-of-mouth rather than star power.

Gram Chikitsalay 2 trailer: Amol Parashar's health mission faces new hurdles The trailer of Gram Chikitsalay 2 sends Dr Prabhat, played by Amol Parashar, back to Bhathkandi, where reviving the Primary Health Centre remains an uphill task. It sets up a season shaped by village politics, distrust and shortages that keep testing rural healthcare.

Did you know 94-year-old Singeetham Srinivasa Rao wrapped Sing Geetham in 77 days? Director Singeetham Srinivasa Rao completed Sing Geetham in 77 shooting days at the age of 94. The film's remote direction, health setback and all-sung narrative underline its unusual production.

Sing Geetham review: A grandfather's tale told by 94-year-old master storytellerRelease Date:Fri Jun 12, 2026 Sing Geetham review: Singeetham Srinivasa Rao's Sing Geetham follows a village where people are cursed to sing every word after greed destroys its last sacred tree. The musical fantasy turns that eccentric premise into a sincere fable about nature, greed and human change.

Misogyny, abuse, clicks: How the Pranit More row exposes stand-up comedy's crisis The controversy around comedian Pranit More's viral crowd-work clip should spark a larger debate about the state of stand-up comedy. As misogyny, profanity and shock value increasingly drive engagement, are jokes being replaced by content designed purely for clicks?

Katrina Kaif eyes comeback with Chandni Bar 2, joins Tabu in sequel: Report Katrina Kaif is reportedly in talks for Madhur Bhandarkar's Chandni Bar 2. The sequel is said to revisit the red light area and crime ecosystem, according to sources.

Singer Chinmayi calls out TVK and DMK over platforming sexual abuser Vedan Singer Chinmayi Sripaada criticised Vijay's party TVK, MLA Rajmohan and DMK for platforming rapper Vedan despite past sexual allegations against him. She said women who speak up against influential men are often left without support while such figures continue to be platformed.

Shahid Kapoor reacts as Anupama Chopra alleges paid negativity against Alia Bhatt Shahid Kapoor has responded after Anupama Chopra's remarks about an alleged paid campaign targeting Alia Bhatt sparked widespread discussion. Here's what the actor said and why the conversation is making headlines.

After confirming political entry, actor Raghava Lawrence asks fans to say yes or no Actor-director Raghava Lawrence shared an 18-minute video on X to explain his political interest and seek a yes-or-no response from fans. Earlier, in an X post, he said that several circumstances had led him to consider entering politics.

Kangana flags Bollywood's 'Pakistan prem', links Ranveer's Don 3 row to Dhurandhar Asked about Ranveer Singh being targeted, Kangana Ranaut linked the chatter to Dhurandhar. She said the reaction reflected discomfort over films that challenge older narratives on Pakistan.

Salman Khan approaches High Court against Kala Hiran, alleges reputation damage Salman Khan has approached the Delhi High Court against Kala Hiran: Battle for Legacy film over alleged use of his persona. He says the film and its promotions could affect ongoing proceedings and damage his reputation.

general news.headline: Indian-crewed ships: Trump counters Centcom's Hellfire missiles with Iran dronesCentcom has acknowledged striking and disabling three Indian-crewed ships near the Gulf of Oman this week. The US forces shared a video of Hellfire missiles hitting one of the ships. President Trump, however, insists the attacks were carried out by Iranian drones.

Canada vs Bosnia Highlights: Super sub Cyle Larin makes it a 1-1 drawCanada (CAN) vs Bosnia and Herzegovina (BIH) Live score and updates, FIFA World Cup 2026: Bosnia came within touching distance of a famous World Cup win after Jovo Lukic's first international goal put them ahead. But Cyle Larin's stunning strike off the bench rescued a point for Canada in an entertaining contest in Toronto.

Iran's Araghchi says deal with US never been closer, Trump reshares his postIn a post on X, Araghchi expressed optimism about the negotiations and urged media outlets to avoid speculation after reports emerged detailing parts of a proposed agreement.

IMD confirms El Nino, warns of intensifying conditions through monsoonFor India, it has historically been linked with weaker monsoon rainfall, higher temperatures, prolonged dry spells and an increased risk of drought in some years.

Himanshu Jangra's boss, the only sane voice in the Rs 370 biryani remark insanityHimanshu Jangra faced immense backlash after describing the 'Rs 370 biryani saga' at Pranit More's show. While his boss Vivek Vishwakarma sacked him, he also mentioned that accountability should still leave room for reflection and change.

Totally unacceptable: Trump blames Iran for attacks on 'Indian' ships in HormuzDonald Trump blamed Iran for attacks on 'Indian' ships in the Strait of Hormuz and criticised Tehran over the proposed peace deal. His comments came as three oil tankers carrying dozens of Indian sailors were caught up in recent US military operations in the region.

Not Justified: Jaishankar raises Indian mariners' deaths with RubioThe conversation comes days after three Indian crew members were killed in a US strike on the Palau-flagged tanker MT Settebello off the coast of Oman, triggering a sharp response from New Delhi. The vessel had 24 Indian crew members on board, of whom 21 were rescued, while the three missing sailors were later confirmed dead.

Women's T20 World Cup Highlights: England take 87-run win in tournament openerEngland (ENG-W) vs Sri Lanka (SL-W) Highlights, Women's T20 World Cup: England stamped their authority on the tournament with a commanding win over Sri Lanka, as Sophie Ecclestone struck twice in the final over to bowl the opposition out and complete a clinical all-round performance.

The Fab Four had giants. Kane Williamson is the one they all lovedHe averaged nearly 50 across formats, won a World Test Championship, and came agonisingly close to a World Cup. But Kane Williamson's greatest contribution to cricket was something no scorecard ever captured.

SpaceX opens at $150 in strong Nasdaq debut, up 11% from IPO priceSpaceX surged more than 20 per cent in its Nasdaq debut, lifting its valuation above USD 2 trillion as investors piled into the world's biggest IPO. The stock opened at USD 150, signalling strong demand and easing concerns over its market debut.

Heavy rain returns to Delhi-NCR for second night, IMD issues red alertDelhi-NCR saw a second straight night of heavy rain, thunderstorms and strong winds on Friday. The IMD issued a red alert and urged caution as the wet spell is likely to continue.

4,000 EVMs gutted in Bengal's govt building fire, SIT probes sabotage claimsKolkata Police have formed an SIT after a fire gutted nearly 4,000 EVMs in an Alipore government building. The blaze's unusual spread and sabotage claims have put the cause under scrutiny.

This Padma Shri scientist proved India carries world's oldest DNA outside AfricaPadma Shri awardee Dr Kumarasamy Thangaraj helped prove that Andaman tribes carry one of the world's oldest human genetic lineages outside Africa. His work changed how scientists understand Indian DNA, medicine, migration and ancestry. Two decades later, GenomeIndia is revealing just how much of India's genetic story the world had overlooked.

Monsoon to stay active along west coast, rain in Delhi-NCR on SaturdayThe southwest monsoon is set to stay active along the west coast on Saturday, with widespread rain likely in Kerala and coastal Karnataka. Scattered showers may also reach Delhi-NCR, parts of the Northeast and several eastern states.

Geneva likely venue for US-Iran peace deal signing; Tehran denies reportsGeneva is being considered as a possible venue for signing a US-Iran peace agreement, but Tehran has dismissed claims that a deal will be signed on Sunday, saying no final decision has been made yet.

Royal family feud turns bloody: Woman shoots husband's second wife inside fortThe first wife of Nagod royal family member Rupendra Singh shot and critically injured his second wife inside the historic Parsamaniya Fort in Madhya Pradesh's Satna district.

Haunted 3D review: Familiar ghost story that works better than expectedRelease Date:Sun Jul 12, 2026Vikram Bhatt's Haunted 3D: Echoes of the Past follows a betrayed director into a cursed palace and a ghostly romance. The film's mystery holds attention, but weak VFX and minimal scares limit its impact.

Compromised PM can't protect sons of Mother India: Rahul Gandhi on sailors' deathsRahul Gandhi accused Prime Minister Narendra Modi of remaining silent on the deaths of three Indian sailors in recent US strikes, alleging that he lacks courage to confront those killed India's sons.

Video: Maharashtra woman stabbed by boyfriend on road over relationship disputeA Palghar District Collector's Office employee was critically injured in a sharp-weapon attack near Gold Cinema. Police are probing a suspected relationship dispute as teams search for the absconding accused.

Evidence was systematically destroyed: Bengal Govt reopens RG Kar case filesWest Bengal's Health Department has reopened the RG Kar case files after the new minister met the victim's parents. The review will focus on alleged evidence destruction and gaps in the investigation.

Misogyny, abuse, clicks: How the Pranit More row exposes stand-up comedy's crisisThe controversy around comedian Pranit More's viral crowd-work clip should spark a larger debate about the state of stand-up comedy. As misogyny, profanity and shock value increasingly drive engagement, are jokes being replaced by content designed purely for clicks?

People are wrong: AI will not take jobs, it will create more jobs, Jeff Bezos saysJeff Bezos has rejected predictions that artificial intelligence will lead to mass job losses, saying instead that the technology will help create more work and could even cause a labour shortage

Woman arrested for sexually assaulting friend's teen daughter, 4th case against herA 25-year-old woman in Kannur has been booked for allegedly sexually assaulting a 16-year-old girl. The case surfaced after the teenager disclosed the incident in counselling, and police are also examining her alleged links to other cases.

Kerala model arrested at Mumbai airport with 11 kg marijuana hidden In luggageCustoms officials arrested Harsha Sunny at Mumbai airport after allegedly finding more than 11 kg of hydroponic marijuana in her luggage. Investigators are now tracing the consignment's destination and possible links to a wider trafficking network.

sports.headline: Ronaldo's dream, Yamal's moment and a World Cup wide open for the taking Former India captain Bhaichung Bhutia shares his World Cup 2026 favourites, backs Cristiano Ronaldo's final quest for glory, discusses Lamine Yamal's rise and explains why India still lags behind Asia's best.

Canadian teen Victoria Mboko out of Wimbledon due to knee injury at Queen's Victoria Mboko's Wimbledon hopes have been dashed after a knee ligament injury at Queen's Club. The Canadian teenager's withdrawal also ends her much-anticipated doubles partnership with Serena Williams.

FIFA explain empty seats after South Korea vs Czechia crowd sparks debate FIFA defended its attendance figure after television images showed empty seats during South Korea's 2-1 win over the Czech Republic in Guadalajara. The explanation has renewed scrutiny of ticket prices, dynamic pricing and fan access at the World Cup.

Thomas Partey ruled out of Ghana's opening game as Canada denies visa application FIFA World Cup 2026: Ghana midfielder Thomas Partey will miss the Black Stars' opening World Cup match against Panama after Canadian authorities refused his visa application, FIFA confirmed, though he remains eligible for Ghana's remaining group-stage fixtures.

Nora Fatehi kickstarts FIFA World Cup in Canada with grand opening ceremony FIFA World Cup 2026: Canadian-born Bollywood superstar Nora Fatehi returned home in style, headlining Toronto's World Cup opening ceremony and debuting her official anthem Siir Siir, turning BMO Field into a celebration before football officially took over.

Canada vs Bosnia Live: Super sub Cyle Larin makes it a 1-1 draw Canada (CAN) vs Bosnia and Herzegovina (BIH) Live score and updates, FIFA World Cup 2026: Bosnia came within touching distance of a famous World Cup win after Jovo Lukic's first international goal put them ahead. But Cyle Larin's stunning strike off the bench rescued a point for Canada in an entertaining contest in Toronto.

Women's T20 World Cup Highlights: England take 87-run win in tournament opener England (ENG-W) vs Sri Lanka (SL-W) Highlights, Women's T20 World Cup: England stamped their authority on the tournament with a commanding win over Sri Lanka, as Sophie Ecclestone struck twice in the final over to bowl the opposition out and complete a clinical all-round performance.

England vs Sri Lanka Live Score, Women's T20 World Cup: Stars ready to light up opening ceremony England (ENG-W) vs Sri Lanka (SL-W) Live Score, Women's T20 World Cup: Excitement is building as the Women's T20 World Cup opening ceremony is set to begin, with fans filling the venue ahead of the tournament's grand launch.

Why Donald Trump won't attend USA's FIFA World Cup opener vs Paraguay US President Donald Trump will not attend the United States' FIFA World Cup opener against Paraguay, despite the country co-hosting the tournament with Mexico and Canada.

Messi has done it, why not Ronaldo? Bhaichung Bhutia picks his FIFA World Cup favourites Speaking to India Today's Harshit Ahuja, former India captain Bhaichung Bhutia picked his favourites for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, while stressing that choosing a single frontrunner is far from straightforward. The football legend said as many as seven or eight teams are genuine contenders and capable of lifting the sport's most prestigious trophy.

Rohit's fitness, Virat's replacement: Morkel spells out India's plans for Afghan ODIs Speaking to the media on the eve of India's ODI series against Afghanistan in Dharamsala, bowling coach Morne Morkel dismissed concerns over Rohit Sharma's fitness and outlined the team's options to replace the injured Virat Kohli at No. 3.

Neeraj Chopra to miss Doha Diamond League, yet to recover from back injuryNeeraj Chopra will miss the Doha Diamond League on June 19 as he continues recovering from a back injury in Switzerland. His absence comes a year after his 90.23m national record in Doha, where another elite javelin field is set to compete.

Virat Kohli congratulates retiring Williamson: Well done mate, life's only just begunVirat Kohli paid an emotional tribute to Kane Williamson following the New Zealand great's retirement from international cricket, praising their friendship and celebrating his remarkable legacy.

The Fab Four had giants. Kane Williamson is the one they all lovedHe averaged nearly 50 across formats, won a World Test Championship, and came agonisingly close to a World Cup. But Kane Williamson's greatest contribution to cricket was something no scorecard ever captured.

India vs Afghanistan 1st ODI: When and where to watch, squads and key updatesIndia move from a one-sided Test win into a three-match ODI series against Afghanistan. The series opens a new season for Shubman Gill's side and gives Afghanistan a first bilateral ODI shot at India.

Kane Williamson retires: New Zealand great walks away on his own termsFormer New Zealand captain Kane Williamson, one of the members of the Fab Four of modern cricket, has announced his retirement. The 35-year-old has hung up his boots midway through a Test series in England.

Don't ignore Rishabh Pant: Ex-pacer warns selectors ahead of 2027 ODI World CupIrfan Pathan has urged selectors not to write off Rishabh Pant after his white-ball omission. He said Pant can still force a comeback before the 2027 ODI World Cup.

Fever to fervour: Oh overcomes illness to inspire South Korea's opening-day winFIFA World Cup 2026: Oh Hyeon-gyu came off the bench to score late as South Korea beat Czechia 2-1 in their opener. The striker said a 38-degree fever nearly ruled him out, while ciacg Hong Myung-bo praised the side's refusal to give up.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has the potential to be bigger than Virat, Sachin: Dale SteynFormer South Africa pacer Dale Steyn believes teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has the potential to surpass even Sachin Tendulkar and Virat Kohli, while warning that India must manage the youngster carefully to help him fulfil his immense promise.

You'll never get me out: Sachin Tendulkar dares daughter Sara on private jetSachin Tendulkar turned a private jet cabin into a playful cricket pitch with his family. The clip travelled fast online, underlining how completely the game still shapes his public image.

FIFA under fire as empty seats overshadow South Korea's comeback winWorld Cup: FIFA reported an attendance of 44,985 for the Group A match between South Korea and the Czech Republic in Guadalajara. Still, large sections of empty seats at Estadio Akron reignited concerns over ticket prices and fan demand at the expanded 48-team tournament.

Opening ceremony: Who is performing, when and where to watchThe ICC Women's T20 World Cup 2026 opens at Edgbaston with a ceremony featuring the full cast of Wicked before England face Sri Lanka. The staging underlines the tournament's scale, with strong ticket sales and a wider push to present women's cricket as a major global event.

WI vs SL, 1st T20I: Holder, Joseph power West Indies to series-opening winJason Holder led the charge with the ball before Shai Hope anchored the chase with an unbeaten 65 as West Indies defeated Sri Lanka by seven wickets in the first T20I at Sabina Park on Thursday to take an early lead in the three-match series.

Real Madrid goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois set to retire after FIFA World Cup 2026FIFA World Cup 2026: Thibaut Courtois has indicated the ongoing World Cup is more likely to be his last tournament with Belgium than not. Any exit would hasten Belgium's transition as younger goalkeepers wait behind a century-capped senior figure.

Brought immense glory to nation: PM Modi condoles shooting legend Jaspal RanaPrime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday expressed grief over the demise of former Asian Games gold medallist Jaspal Rana, describing it as a major loss for Indian sport and paying tribute to his outstanding contributions as both a shooter and coach.

Gwalior's Yashbardhan Chauhan to lead India U-19 on Sri Lanka tourGwalior's Yashvardhan Singh Chauhan will lead India Under-19 on the Sri Lanka tour. His appointment, along with Manal Chauhan's selection, has lifted Chambal cricket and underlined the region's growing pipeline.

Australian Open: PV Sindhu dismantles Chen Su Yu to book semifinal berthThird seed PV Sindhu produced a dominant display to enter the women's singles semi-finals of the Australian Open Super 500, defeating Chinese Taipei's Chen Su Yu 21-6, 21-9 on Friday.

Raul Jimenez lives his World Cup moment after defying the impossibleFIFA World Cup 2026: Six years after a skull fracture threatened his life and career, Raul Jimenez scored his first World Cup goal in Mexico's home opener. It was a moment that transcended football, completing one of the game's most remarkable comeback stories.

Indian shooting legend and coach Jaspal Rana dies at 49Renowned Indian shooting coach and former Asian Games gold medallist Jaspal Rana died at the age of 49 after suffering cardiac complications that first surfaced during his return journey from Munich to Delhi following the ISSF World Cup.

World Cup: Hwang In-beom fires South Korea to 2-1 comeback victory over CzechiaSouth Korea produced a stirring comeback to defeat Czechia 2-1 in their FIFA World Cup Group A opener in Guadalajara on Thursday, with Hwang In-beom starring in a memorable second-half turnaround.

England braces for history as Women's T20 World Cup promises a blockbusterThe 2026 Women's T20 World Cup promises to be a landmark event, with 12 teams competing in England and Wales. While Australia start as favourites, Harmanpreet Kaur's India, hosts England, defending champions New Zealand and South Africa are expected to be strong contenders.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
